title: Collecting Artist Trading Cards - a fun and affordable hobby
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kuoLAhyeaJQ

Daniel (@dungeondive) walks through one of fantasy gaming's best-kept secrets: artist trading cards aren't supposed to be valuable, which somehow makes them genuinely affordable. Flip through sets from legends—Michael Whan's nostalgia-soaked mysticism, Ken Kelly's bulging barbarian machismo, Frank Frisetta's transcendent flesh-painting—and you're looking at maybe $15 to $40 per complete set of ninety cards.

These aren't investments; they're visual escapism. Danny's collection sprawls across multiple binders showcasing everything from Lovecraft cover art to weird space-cowboys, perfect for sparking solo RPG encounters or simply feeding your dopamine receptors with remembered book store browsing. The flavor text on each card adds unexpected depth, and seeing an image connected to a book you actually own closes some nostalgic loop in the brain.

The hobby flourished during the pandemic as a quiet refuge from reality, requiring nothing but a thumb and a binder page. Unlike Magic cards or Pokemon madness, these won't break the bank, which liberates you to actually *enjoy* the art instead of obsessing over grading.

----

Are you a collector of anything that scratches a similar itch? What draws you to physical objects tied to worlds you love?
